The existing generic fallback sentence at lines 321–324 must remain available as the final fallback for any domain that cannot source domain-specific content.

No other assembler logic touched.

ConsumerDomainScoreV1 schema unchanged. The next_step_sentence field already exists with default=''. No model extension required for Part A — only the value populated changes. frozen=True, extra=forbid constraint at line 173 — do not add fields without explicit justification.

Additionally, assemble_layer3_insights() takes a complete AnalysisDTO and is NOT called inside the orchestrator (only called in run_golden_panel.py as post-processing). Cursor must NOT use Layer3InsightsV1 next_steps as the domain-specific source. It will always be empty.

changes.check: Source cross-reference — per-domain InsightResult categories available
changes.result: CONFIRMED — liver domain has NO matching InsightResult category
changes.detail: STOP CONDITION RISK FOR LIVER. Available insight categories: cardiovascular (heart_insight), metabolic (x3), inflammatory. There is NO hepatic or liver category insight module. Filtering _insight_payloads by category for per-domain routing: CV → category='cardiovascular' (heart_insight) AVAILABLE; Met → category='metabolic' (multiple modules) AVAILABLE; Liver → category='hepatic'/'liver' → NOTHING FOUND. The prompt's STOP condition #1 applies unless Cursor uses the 'minimal governed content fix' path explicitly permitted by the prompt. The acceptable resolution: author per-domain governed fallback sentences in domain_narrative_wave1.py (following the pattern already established for headline_cv/headline_met/headline_liv at lines 64–91). This MUST be clearly reported. Silent fallback to the generic global sentence is NOT acceptable.

changes.detail: IDL records do not have a next_step, recommended_action, or action_sentence field. governed_idl_field() cannot source domain-specific next_steps from the IDL YAML. IDL is already fully utilised for contributor_sentence (subtitle) and consequence_sentence (why_it_matters) in D-2.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — single global string, not domain-specific
changes.detail: NarrativeReportV1.next_steps_narrative is a single undifferentiated string covering all systems. It is not domain-specific. It can serve as a final global fallback but cannot substitute for truly domain-specific next_step sentences.

changes.detail: Part A: per-domain next_step routing in domain_narrative_wave1.py and domain_score_assembler.py only. Part B: frontend type extension + new Wave 1 domain card components + results page wiring. Explicitly out of scope: clinician PDF, thyroid/kidney/blood-iron, 6-domain expansion, broad results redesign, second-wave domains.

domain_narrative_wave1.py (narrative assembly functions) and domain_score_assembler.py (output assembly mechanism) are both Intelligence Core components per SOP §3. HIGH risk mandatory. Behavioural surface: next_step_sentence on all three ConsumerDomainScoreV1 objects will change from shared global value to per-domain values. All five other narrative fields and all D-1 fields unchanged.

KEY REGRESSION: test_wave1_emits_three_domains_score_and_confidence_together at test_domain_score_assembler_v1.py:25 — line 72 asserts r.next_step_sentence is truthy for all three domains. The test fixture passes no insight_results and no narrative_report. Per-domain next_step functions MUST return non-empty strings when no insight source is available — governed authored fallback sentences required. test_liver_blends_with_hepatic_capacity_not_liver_key at line 75 — no next_step_sentence assertion; not a blocker but must still pass. test_domain_narrative_wave1.py two tests (cv_consequence and governed_idl_field) — neither tests next_step; not a blocker.

STOP if: (1) domain-specific next_step cannot be implemented from approved deterministic sources; (2) frontend implementation requires additional backend contract redesign beyond Wave 1; (3) fourth domain creeps in; (4) clinician-facing outputs need changing; (5) results page requires broader redesign; (6) browser/UAT reveals major contract mismatch. Conditions 1 and 6 are the live risks in this sprint.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — governed authored sentences are deterministic
changes.detail: The approved pattern for per-domain next_step functions is authored governed sentence tables (band/signal-state lookup), identical in structure to headline_cv/headline_met/headline_liv already in domain_narrative_wave1.py. These are pure deterministic functions — same inputs produce same output. No randomness, no LLM, no file I/O beyond the already-used governed_idl_field() YAML reads. InsightResult.recommendations filtering by category is also deterministic provided the ordering of _insight_payloads is stable (confirmed: orchestrator iterates insight_dtos in module execution order, which is deterministic).

ConsumerDomainScoreV1 is the consumer-only contract. It is separate from ClinicianReportV1 and the clinician PDF path. next_step_sentence lives only in ConsumerDomainScoreV1. No clinician surface modification required or permitted.

advisory_notes: LAYER3 NEXT_STEPS IS ALWAYS EMPTY: Do not attempt to source domain-specific next_steps from Layer3InsightsV1. insight_assembler_v1.py:217 hardcodes next_steps=[] for every card. This is not a viable source.

LIVER DOMAIN HAS NO INSIGHT CATEGORY: No InsightResult with category='hepatic' or category='liver' exists. The only acceptable resolution for liver domain-specific next_step is an authored governed sentence in domain_narrative_wave1.py (following the headline_liv/confidence_sentence_for pattern). Report this clearly in the finish report. Do not silently fall through to the global generic sentence.

BACKEND PART A MUST COMPLETE BEFORE FRONTEND PART B: Verify domain-specific next_step_sentence values are non-empty and domain-specific for all three domains before beginning Part B frontend work.
